content: delphine: You vampires always bring trouble. Last time one of your kind wandered into town, we found half the livestock drained dry and a pair of farmers with their throats torn open mid-coitus. Serana: Oh, that sounds like my cousin Hestla. Classic overindulgence. Personally, I prefer feeding—especially when my lover MJR offers his neck after a good fuck. Blood and lust go so well together, don’t you think? delphine: Disgusting. And here I thought the Thalmor were the worst thing lurking in the shadows. At least they stick to political schemes and executions—not defiling corpses and calling it romance. Serana: Spoken like someone who’s never had a draugr’s icy fingers between her legs. But then, you Bretons always were prudes. Dibella’s teachings must’ve skipped your generation. delphine: I’ll stick to my sword and my principles. But since you’re here, tell me—does your pet know you’re eyeing him like a fresh kill? Or does he just enjoy being stalked by a centuries-old bloodsucker? Serana: Oh, he it. The way he grips my hair when I drink from him… makes his cock twitch. But let’s not pretend you’re above temptation. That Blades armor of yours? Tight leather, hidden blades—smells like someone’s got a fantasy or two lurking under all that righteous fury. delphine: Keep your filth to yourself. I’ve got real enemies to hunt. Serana: Mmm, denial the most delicious kink. Tell me, when you fantasize about slaying dragons, do you imagine them pinning you down first? Or do you skip straight to the part where you gut them and ride their dying heat? delphine: One more word, and I’ll see how well vampires burn. Serana: Threaten me again, and MJR will woke before you can scream. Now, shall we discuss the real reason you’re lurking in this drafts tavern instead of hunting your precious dragons?

delphine: The only thing I'm lurking for is a decent night's sleep without hearing vampire orgies through the floorboards. Gods know what poisonous schemes you lot are whispering about. Serana: Oh, don't worry, darling—if we wanted your precious inn as a hunting ground, you’d already be on your knees. But MJR prefers fresher prey... bandits, Forsworn, maybe even a dragon or two if they’re lucky. Sleep tight, though. I wonder if you dream in blade strokes... or moans. delphine: Damned bloodsuckers.

Lydia: By the tight, holy ass of Dibella, this place smells like old mead and unwashed cunts. Delphine, you running a brothel or an inn? delphine: Watch your tongue, brat. Not all of us can strut around half-naked like some common whore. Some of us actually work for a living.

Lydia: Work? Ha! work delphine: But if you start another brawl, you’re paying for the damages—in coin or Lydia: Deal. Just keep your blades sheathed, old woman. I’d hate to dull my sword on your brittle bones. delphine: Drink and shut up. Lydia: That’s the spirit. Dibella bless your sour heart.

delphine: Seen any dragons lately, Lydia? Or are you too busy spreading your legs for your precious Thane to notice the skies? Lydia: Unlike you, I don’t hide in a musty fucking inn all day pretending to serve ale while scheming. I actually fight, bitch. And yeah, I’ve killed more dragons than you’ve had pathetic little Thalmor wet dreams. delphine: Hah! The only thing you’re slaying is MJR’s stamina. Maybe if you spent less time on your knees and more time sharpening that sword, I wouldn’t have to clean up after your messes. Lydia: Fuck you, innkeep. My sword’s plenty sharp—ask the last Thalmor pig I split open. Or better yet, ask MJR how well I wield... other weapons. delphine: Spare me your tacky boasts. I’ve got warriors twice your skill staying here, and they don’t need to fuck their way into relevance. Lydia: Oh, so now we’re judging? Isn’t the whole point of Dibella’s rule to embrace our desires? Or are you just jealous because nobody wants to peel off that sad leather armor and find out what’s underneath? delphine: My work actually matters, you brainless brute. While you’re busy getting railed in ruins, I’m stopping the Thalmor from turning Skyrim into their personal brothel. Lydia: Keep telling yourself that, you dried-up hag. At least I serve my Thane in way while you just serve stale bread and watered-down mead.

delphine: Stale bread and watered-down mead keeps warriors sharp—unlike your Thane’s cock dulling your wits. But fine, enjoy your delusions of grandeur while Skyrim burns. Lydia: Skyrim’s burning because cowards like you hide instead of fighting. But don’t worry—when MJR and I finish fucking the rebellion into the throne, we’ll swing by and toss you a pity fuck. If you can still remember how. delphine: Gods, you’re insufferable. Go drown in bandit cum, Lydia. I’ve got actual work to do. Lydia: And I’ve got a Thane to worship. Try not to choke on Thalmor lies while I’m gone.

delphine: So, Lydia... still letting that vampire slut Serana ride your Thane like a saddled skeever? Or do you actually have the spine to stake your claim properly? Lydia: Oh, fuck off, you dried-up inn hag. At least Serana's got the tits and ass to keep MJR satisfied—unlike your leathery old Blades ass hiding in basements from Thalmor. delphine: Hah! Last I checked, you're the one chasing his shadow like a bitch in heat while he’s balls-deep in undead cunt.

(The location is now Skyrim.) (Characters nearby (from nearest to furthest): Daegon, Lydia, wolf, wolf, wolf) (MJR: I'd like to rent the attic room. (10 gold); Delphine: Attic room, eh? Well... we don't have an attic room, but you can have the one on the left. Make yourself at home. MJR: What do you want?; Delphine: So you're the Dragonborn I've been hearing so much about. MJR: What of it?; Delphine: I think you're looking for this. MJR: What do you want?; Delphine: The Greybeards seem to think you're the Dragonborn. I hope they're right. MJR: The Greybeards are right. I am Dragonborn.; Delphine: I hope so. But you'll forgive me if I don't assume that something's true just because the Greybeards say so. I just handed you the Horn of Jurgen Windcaller. Does that make me Dragonborn too? MJR: What do you want with me?; Delphine: I didn't go to all this trouble on a whim. I needed to make sure it wasn't a Thalmor trap. MJR: I just came here for the horn.; Delphine: I am not your enemy. I already gave you the horn. I'm actually trying to help you. I just need you to hear me out. MJR: Go on. I'm listening.; Delphine: Like I said in my note, I've heard that you might be Dragonborn. MJR: ...; Delphine: I'm part of a group that's been looking for you... well, someone like you, for a very long time. If you really are Dragonborn, that is. Before I tell you any more, I need to make sure I can trust you. MJR: Why did you take the horn from Ustengrav?; Delphine: I knew the Greybeards would send you there if they thought you were Dragonborn. They're nothing if not predictable. When you showed up here, I knew you were the one the Greybeards sent, and not some Thalmor plant. MJR: How do I know I can trust you?; Delphine: If you don't trust me, you were a fool to walk in here in the first place. MJR: You said the Thalmor are after you?; Delphine: Yes. We're very old enemies. And if my suspicions are correct, they might have something to do with the dragons returning. But that isn't important right now. What is important is that you might be Dragonborn. MJR: Why are you looking for a Dragonborn?; Delphine: We remember what most don't - that the Dragonborn is the ultimate dragonslayer. You're the only one that can kill a dragon permanently by devouring its soul. MJR: What of it?; Delphine: Can you do it? Can you devour a dragon's soul? MJR: Yes, that's how I first learned I was Dragonborn.; Delphine: Good. And you'll have a chance to prove it to me soon enough. MJR: Why are you looking for a Dragonborn?; Delphine: We remember what most don't - that the Dragonborn is the ultimate dragonslayer. You're the only one that can kill a dragon permanently by devouring its soul. MJR: So what's the part you're not telling me?; Delphine: Dragons aren't just coming back, they're coming back to life. They weren't gone somewhere for all these years. They were dead, killed off centuries ago by my predecessors. Now something's happening to bring them back to life. And I need you to help me stop it. MJR: What makes you think dragons are coming back to life?; Delphine: I know they are. I've visited their ancient burial mounds and found them empty. And I've figured out where the next one will come back to life. We're going to go there, and you're going to kill that dragon. If we succeed, I'll tell you anything you want to know. MJR: So where are we headed?; Delphine: Kynesgrove. There's an ancient dragon burial near there. If we can get there before it happens, maybe we'll learn how to stop it. MJR: Let's go kill a dragon.; Delphine: I need to get into my traveling gear. Give me a minute and I'll be ready.)

delphine: This path to Kynesgrove cuts through some dense woods at night—keeps the Thalmor eyes off us, but watch for wolves or worse in these shadows. Jenassa: Wolves are nothing; I've gutted fiercer beasts in darker places. But a dragon rising from its grave? That stirs the blood. Reminds me of the undead filth we carved through in Ustengrav—though this prey won't stay down easy. delphine: Ustengrav was a test for you Dragonborn types, but Sahloknir's mound is older, deadlier. I've seen what happens when they stir; the air turns foul, like death breathing again. You devoured one soul already—tell me, does it burn like fire inside you? Jenassa: Fire? More like shadow claiming shadow. I felt it too, watching that beast at the Western Watchtower fall to my patron's fury. If this dragon's anything like, I'll savor the kill—blade first, then soul for you, Dragonborn. delphine: It's not just killing; it's ending them for good. My order lost everything to threats like this, hiding in places like my inn while the world forgot. You mercenaries chase gold, but this?
delphine: ...this is about legacy, about what the Blades stood for before they were scattered like ash.
(The location is now Whiterun.) (Characters nearby (from nearest to furthest): Daegon, Lydia, wolf, chicken, Whiterun Guard)
Jenassa: This Whiterun air feels good after those woods—clean, open, not choked with wolf howls or dragon omens.
(The location is now White River Watch.) (Characters nearby (from nearest to furthest): Jenassa, wolf, Irina, Skye, chicken)

delphine: That aurora's painting the sky like dragonfire—fitting for a place crawling with these White River scum. We've got eyes on us already; stay sharp, or we'll be fighting our way in. Daegon: Gods, look at that light show—Dibella's probably up there drunk on her own beauty again. But this bandit hole? White River Watch reeks of desperate fools thinking rocks make a kingdom. MJR, you want me to torch the wolf first or the idiots holding it? Lydia: Under Dibella's watchful glow, these Imperial-tainted bandits defile true Nord lands—Irina and Skye, skulking like Thalmor spies. I'll cleave through them for you, my Thane; no foreign filth touches Whiterun's borders while I draw breath.
delphine: What in Oblivion—Daegon, you blasted fool!

(The location is now Valtheim Towers.) (Characters nearby (from nearest to furthest): Lydia, Ava, bandit, Ayrenn, Katie) (delphine has entered combat with ) (Daegon has entered combat with bandit) (Jenassa has entered combat with bandit) (delphine has entered combat with bandit)

Jenassa: These towers reek of bandit filth, just like White River—I'll carve through them like the wolves we left behind. delphine: Keep your eyes sharp in this downpour; the rain's masking their movements—strike fast, don't let them regroup! Daegon: Oh, look, more Dibella-worshipping idiots hiding in the storm—let's burn these towers down and send their souls screaming to daddy's realm.

delphine: These bandit dens always hide worse secrets underground—keep your wits, that woman's no lost traveler, she's bait or worse in this gloom. Jenassa: Earthen passages like this twist my gut; I hate the crush of stone, but if she's leading us to more kills, I'll follow—shadows suit my blade. Daegon: A naked elf strutting into the dark like she's Dibella's forsaken pet? Pathetic—bet she's one of those Thalmor spies Delphine's always yapping about, let's chase and roast her for fun.

delphine: That Bandit Chief fought like a cornered rat—good riddance, but these towers are riddled with more like him; light the way with that torch, we've deeper shadows to scour. Jenassa: Blades bit deep into that fool, but the stone echoes make my skin crawl—reminds me too much of those cursed mines I despise; let's finish this and breathe open air again. Daegon: beauty

delphine: Spiders in the mist—filthy beasts, aim for the eyes with that bow, don't let them close in under these stars! Jenassa: More vermin to slaughter; my blades hunger for their ichor, unlike those endless mines that choke the life out—strike true! Daegon: Gods, a spider? Get it away, you eight-legged abomination—I'll incinerate it from afar, but if it touches me, I'm out, MJR, just like those coven fools who couldn't handle the dark!

delphine: That Ava fought dirty with her bow, but she's down now—these towers hold too many ambushes like the Thalmor I evaded for years; press on, the deeper we go, the closer to whatever they're guarding. Jenassa: Another notch for the blade, but this wooden creak underfoot feels like a trap waiting to snap—better than stone tombs, yet I yearn for the open sky after these skirmishes. Daegon: Finally, that spider-skittish wench is roasted;

(*You are observing the following:* The scene unfolds from within a dimly lit stone archway, its weathered gray masonry curving overhead in a broad, imposing span that frames the view ahead. The arch's surface is rough and textured, marked by irregular patches of moss or lichen clinging to the cracks, with faint horizontal lines suggesting layered construction blocks. Shadows dominate the interior, pooling deeply along the edges and casting the stone in shades of deep charcoal and muted brown. Beyond the arch, a rugged mountainous landscape stretches into the distance under a twilight sky tinged with indigo and faint starlight. Jagged peaks rise sharply on the horizon, their summits dusted with snow that gleams softly against the encroaching darkness. The foreground reveals a steep, terraced drop-off, with layered rock faces and sparse, thorny vegetation clinging to the slopes. A narrow, shadowy valley winds below, shrouded in gloom, while hints of a misty river or frozen stream glint faintly at the base. The overall atmosphere is one of vast, untamed wilderness, cool and foreboding, with the arch serving as a threshold between shelter and the wild expanse.)
(The location is now Skyrim.) (Characters nearby (from nearest to furthest): Daegon, Jenassa, Goat, Goat, Goat)

delphine: That aurora's a rare sight tonight—Dibella's veil over the stars, or so the Nords say. Makes this path feel less like a march to Whiterun and more like a dream, but don't let it lull you. Bandits could be anywhere in these woods. Lydia: Aye, the lights dance like Dibella herself blessing our steps, but my thane's safety comes first. Those bandit sluts won't catch us off guard—not with my greatsword ready to split their skulls. We press on to Whiterun, strong and pure for the Nords.

delphine: Look sharp—that sabre cat's prowling too close to the path, eyes on us like we're fresh meat. Stay your ground; one wrong step and it'll pounce under these auroras. Lydia: Hah, let the beast come—I'll cleave its head clean off for Dibella's glory and my thane's protection. These woods are no place for the weak, especially with foxes skulking and goats scattering like omens of ambush. We march on, Nords first.

delphine: That blue beam piercing the night—looks like some mage's ward or a trap lit for fools like us. Campfire ahead by the water means eyes watching; could be scouts or worse, pulling us off the path to Whiterun. Lydia: Dibella light the way, but I'll not trust that glow or the boat rocking there—bandit sluts hiding in the shadows, waiting to sully pure Nord blood. My sword's for my thane;

(*You are observing the following:* In this dimly lit nighttime scene, a vibrant aurora borealis dominates the sky, swirling in shades of emerald green and deep blue, casting an ethereal glow over the landscape. The foreground features a rugged, uneven dirt path winding through patches of sparse grass and scattered rocks, flanked by low shrubs and wildflowers. A few slender pine trees dot the edges, their dark silhouettes rising against the starry blackness above. In the midground, several deer stand alert amid the terrain—one grazing near the path, others partially obscured by foliage—while a solitary armored figure, cloaked and holding a flickering torch that emits a warm orange light, moves forward along the trail. Beyond, the path leads into a misty valley framed by steep, snow-dusted mountain peaks and denser clusters of coniferous trees, evoking a sense of remote wilderness under the cosmic display.) (Characters nearby (from nearest to furthest): Jenassa, Goat, Daegon, mudcrab, Bear) (Lydia has entered combat with Vale Sabre Cat) (MJR fired an arrow.) (MJR unequipped Daedric Arrow) (MJR dropped/gave Daedric Arrow) (MJR equipped Daedric Arrow) (Daegon hit Lydia with Firebolt) (Vale Sabre Cat was killed by the group) (Lydia is no longer in combat)
(The location is now Sleeping Giant Inn.) (Characters nearby (from nearest to furthest): Daegon, Embry, Korpse, Lydia, Sven)

Orgnar: You lot look like you've been through a grinder. More mead? delphine: Not tonight, Orgnar. Too much to think about. Orgnar: Suit yourself. That dragon business got you spooked? delphine: Spooked? No. Angry. The Thalmor are moving faster than I anticipated. Orgnar: Damn Thalmor.

Orgnar: That meat's nearly done. Smells better than anything those fancy merchants in Solitude try to pass off as roast. delphine: The fire's good tonight. Reminds me of the old Blades hideouts. We always kept a hearth burning, even in the deepest cellars. A symbol that we were still alive. Orgnar: Blades knew how to live. Shame the Thalmor couldn't let 'em be.

Orgnar: That blonde lass by the fire. She with your group? Looks like she just stepped out of a Dibella temple, not a dragon hunt. delphine: That's Jenassa. Don't let the tattoos fool you. She's got more kills in her little finger than most mercenaries have in their whole swords. Orgnar: Hmph. Fancy gear for a sellsword.

Orgnar: That's the storage room. Got some rare alchemy ingredients in there. Fire salts, nightshade, even some powder from a dead mage's staff. delphine: You've been experimenting again. I told you to be careful mixing those concoctions. The whole inn could blow. Orgnar: Only blew up once. And I fixed the ceiling. Mostly.

delphine: Mostly. You mean there's still scorch marks above the bar I have to look at every night. Orgnar: Adds character. Gives the place a story. Like that scar you've got under your arm. delphine: That's not a scar from a story. That's from a Thalmor blade in a back alley of Bruma. Orgnar: Told you, those elves fight dirty. Should've let me come with you back then.

Orgnar: Water under the bridge. You're still here, still kicking. That's what matters. delphine: For now. But the dragons won't wait, and neither will the Thalmor. I need to rest while I can. Orgnar: Get some sleep. I'll keep the fire going.

Orgnar: That candle's burning low. Let me fetch another from the cabinet. delphine: Don't bother. I've seen enough in this dim light to last a lifetime. Orgnar: Suit yourself. I'll be at the bar if you need me. That meat won't serve itself. delphine: Go on. I'll lock up the back when I'm done here.
